epic.title: Fix Da Yun branch audit in module1_macro.py
deliverables: Correct priority ordering for Da Yun branch audit (三会 > 三合 > 冲 > 六合 > 半合 > 刑 > 害 > 破)
Add missing interaction types (三会 and 半合 checks)
Apply graduated magnitudes for Da Yun branch interactions
Ensure no short-circuiting on first natal branch (scan all 4 branches per type)

user_stories.story: As a Bazi system auditor, I want the Da Yun branch audit to use the correct canonical priority ordering and include all 8 interaction types so that the macro audit is accurate.
acceptance_criteria: Verification that 三会 > 三合 > 冲 > 六合 > 半合 > 刑 > 害 > 破 priority is respected.
Verification that 三会 and 半合 checks are correctly implemented.
Verification that all 4 natal branches are scanned per interaction type without early breaking.
definition_of_done: Priority check logic implemented in src2/engine/module1_macro.py
No NameError/KeyError for CHONG, LIU_HE, SAN_HE, SAN_HUI, BAN_HE, XING, HAI, PO
Refactored code complies with ruff formatting and checks

user_stories.story: As a Bazi system auditor, I want Da Yun branch interactions to scale with graduated magnitudes and reflect 用神/忌神 polarity correctly so that auspicious/inauspicious signs are accurate.
acceptance_criteria: Verification that magnitudes are graduated (+20 for 三会, +15 for 三合, +10 for 六合, +5 for 半合, -15 for 冲, -8 for 刑/害/破).
Verification that clashing 忌神 results in positive relief and combining 忌神 results in negative impact.
Verification that _unified_medicine / _unified_taboo sets are reused correctly.
definition_of_done: Polarity calculations implemented using sign and polarity multiplier formulation.
No legacy impact dict edits or changes to annual Tai Sui section.
Code in src2/engine/module1_macro.py implements priority-sorted scan of all 8 interaction types.
No breaks on first natal branch (all 4 checked).
No syntax or linting errors are reported by ruff check/format.
acceptance_criteria: Da Yun branch audit logic rewritten from lines 216-249.
Existing imports/variables updated without new model definitions.
Imports and logic compile and pass ruff checks.

cells.criterion: Da Yun interactions checked in order 三会 > 三合 > 冲 > 六合 > 半合 > 刑 > 害 > 破
All 8 types including 三会 and 半合 are active

draft.summary: Fix priority order, missing interactions, magnitude mapping, and polarity wiring in the Da Yun branch audit section of module1_macro.py.

subtasks.title: Rewrite Da Yun branch audit in module1_macro.py
subtasks.instruction: Replace the sequential if-blocks under Da Yun branch audit (lines 216-249) with a single priority-sorted scan of all 8 interaction types: 三会 (+20), 三合 (+15), 冲 (-15), 六合 (+10), 半合 (+5), 刑 (-8), 害 (-8), 破 (-8). Look up the elements for these combinations/targets and apply the polarity logic: for harm types (冲, 刑, 破, 害), taboo is flipped to positive (+), medicine stays negative (-); for combine types (三会, 三合, 六合, 半合), taboo is flipped to negative (-), medicine/neutral stays positive (+). Scan all 4 natal branches per type before deciding the winning category (the highest-priority match wins). Re-use _unified_medicine and _unified_taboo sets already calculated at lines 343-348. Run `ruff check` and `ruff format` on the file to ensure clean compliance.
subtasks.acceptance: Ruff check and format pass, all 8 interaction types checked in correct priority order, correct polarity formula implemented, and all natal branches scanned.

evidence.content: File contains the Da Yun branch audit under lines 216-249 using ty_branch and natal_branches. Needs replacement with priority checking and polarity formula.

risks: Risk of NameError or KeyError if constants (CHONG, LIU_HE, SAN_HE, SAN_HUI, BAN_HE, XING, HAI, PO) are structured differently; mitigated by dynamic type-checking and robust fallback helpers.
Risk of syntax errors during prompt injection; mitigated by using ruff validation in code review.

strategy.how_to_fix: We will rewrite the Da Yun branch audit in src2/engine/module1_macro.py by implementing helper functions to dynamically extract elements for combinations and branches, then looping through the 8 interaction types in canonical priority order. If any of the 4 natal branches matches, we apply the magnitude and polarity math to compute the final ty_branch_impact, and stop scanning lower-priority types.
